message:
style: rustfmt import grouping sweep + as-cast -> try_from hardening across harness

Purely mechanical hygiene across the ffs-core / ffs-xattr / ffs-
harness surfaces - no behaviour changes, driven by rustfmt's
import-group re-ordering and a clippy cast_possible_truncation
tightening.

Rustfmt import grouping:

- crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs, crates/ffs-xattr/src/lib.rs: re-sort
  `use` blocks so type/const names come before lower-snake function
  names within each path, matching the cargo-fmt output in the
  rest of the workspace. Same re-exports, same import surface.
- crates/ffs-xattr/src/lib.rs also re-indents the
  `// e2fsprogs/debugfs leaves h_hash unset` comment to sit on
  column 4 rather than column 54, where the prior trailing-comment
  formatting had drifted.

as-cast -> try_from conversions (safer by construction - the cast
cannot silently truncate):

- crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:
  * `mapping.physical as usize` -> usize::try_from(mapping.physical)
    .expect("physical offset should fit usize")
  * `raw_name.len() as u8` (two occurrences) ->
    u8::try_from(raw_name.len()).expect("... name length fits u8")
  * `payload.len() as u32` for fs.read -> u32::try_from(...)
  * append_send_stream_command's `payload_len as u32` -> same form
  * Drops the now-unnecessary #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)].
- crates/ffs-harness/tests/btrfs_kernel_reference.rs,
  crates/ffs-harness/tests/ext4_journal_recovery.rs,
  crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs,
  crates/ffs-harness/tests/kernel_reference.rs: same pattern
  applied wherever test helpers had `len() as u8 / u16 / u32`, so
  clippy -D warnings stays clean across the test suite.
- crates/ffs-harness/src/btrfs_capability_drift.rs: same
  conversions for the drift-harness helpers.

Every .expect() message spells out which invariant makes the
conversion safe (so a fuzzed regression that trips one of them
produces a readable panic rather than a nameless TryFromIntError).
